## Collection: `artifacts/clwhq-001/public/data/missions`

**Sample Document ID:** `CkPRzEfYJxGNL3RSeRB9`

**Fields:**

```json
{
  "title": "i want to increase the social media presence of my laptop refurbishment company",
  "metadata": {},
  "tab_slug": "online-marketing-orchestrator",
  "tab_id": "blp7imxbp",
  "approvePlanBeforeLaunch": true,
  "autoPilotMode": false,
  "ownerId": "XZSHEes11xf210cRhQDobK0tWOh2",
  "progress": 0,
  "createdAt": {
    "_seconds": 1777552650,
    "_nanoseconds": 142000000
  },
  "exitCode": 0,
  "endTime": {
    "_seconds": 1777552729,
    "_nanoseconds": 668000000
  },
  "error": null,
  "startTime": {
    "_seconds": 1777554113,
    "_nanoseconds": 222000000
  },
  "plan_doc": "# 🎯 Strategic Deployment Plan: Social Presence Growth for Laptop Refurbishment Company\n\n## 📝 Executive Summary\nBuild a consistent, trust-focused social presence that showcases refurbished laptop quality, affordability, and real customer value across LinkedIn, Facebook, and Instagram. The strategy combines educational content, before-and-after transformations, customer proof, and promotional campaigns to increase brand awareness, engagement, and qualified inquiries.\n\n## 📊 Sector Allocations & Targeting\n- **Primary Sector**: Refurbished Technology Retail\n- **Channels**: LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram\n- **Campaign Vibe**: Trustworthy, Practical, Professional\n\n## 🚀 Phased Task Breakdown\n### Phase 1: Content Generation\nCreate a repeatable content engine built around expertise, proof, and buyer confidence.\n- **Task 1**: Build LinkedIn authority content calendar - Assigned to `linkedin-manager` - Create a 4-week content plan featuring business benefits of refurbished laptops, sustainability angles, buying guides, and workplace IT cost-saving insights.\n- **Task 2**: Create Facebook community engagement content - Assigned to `facebook-manager` - Develop posts focused on deals, customer stories, laptop care tips, and trust-building FAQs for local buyers and families.\n- **Task 3**: Produce Instagram visual storytelling content - Assigned to `instagram-manager` - Design a content stream around before-and-after refurbishments, setup reels, product highlights, and short educational carousels.\n\n### Phase 2: Deployment & Analytics\nLaunch consistently, test messaging, and optimize using engagement and inquiry signals.\n- **Task 4**: Launch multi-platform posting cadence - Assigned to `facebook-manager` - Schedule and coordinate an initial 4-week rollout with 3 to 5 posts per week, adapting hooks and captions per platform.\n- **Task 5**: Monitor LinkedIn engagement and lead signals - Assigned to `linkedin-manager` - Track post performance, identify high-interest topics, and refine content toward B2B buyers, startups, and professionals.\n- **Task 6**: Optimize Instagram and Facebook conversion content - Assigned to `instagram-manager` - Review top-performing visual and promotional formats, then iterate on CTA style, product presentation, and audience-focused messaging.",
  "tasksSpawned": true,
  "status": "In-Progress",
  "updatedAt": {
    "_seconds": 1777554604,
    "_nanoseconds": 364000000
  }
}
```

---

  "agentId": "facebook-manager",
  "ownerId": "lZ2LjvHmhGgeAqNxMdkc3U058vh2",
  "content": "CA Intermediate Group 2: Focus on concepts first.\n\n• Costing formats and problem solving\n• Audit standards and reporting basics\n• FM formulas and application\n\nWhen concepts are clear, revision gets easier.",
  "mediaUrl": null,
  "targetUrn": "857004990830855",
  "targetName": "CAsutra",
  "targetPic": "https://scontent-sin11-1.xx.fbcdn.net/v/t39.30808-1/587181505_122103289239122835_711689306366753488_n.png?stp=cp0_dst-png_s50x50&_nc_cat=105&ccb=1-7&_nc_sid=f907e8&_nc_ohc=ssNJJve3Bj4Q7kNvwHYTiNn&_nc_oc=AdpxkT2-IYOUhxRuVR2W2_JvWKn7KFg_qF-J4l-sgwEP9sQ0ONNkRwB8Ad2_olzSyiyk_8trpInzO82NdqohIyV1&_nc_zt=24&_nc_ht=scontent-sin11-1.xx&edm=AGaHXAAEAAAA&_nc_gid=Bw9oH2Lnxgi-sr23-WxW9A&_nc_tpa=Q5bMBQEb-0gwRNQq1LvCvViVz2N_qY_BVPntY6I0jMWDdW73KOCRht_3vFapjVlKVwoAvc_SH5p27alzbA&oh=00_Af3UGb30nYFlGdYlfdU0V70DVG9bLU8ZJ6Iz2aqVUxGwvA&oe=69F65EEF",
  "scheduledAt": {
    "_seconds": 1777544213,

  },

## Collection: `artifacts/clwhq-001/userConfigs`

**Sample Document ID:** `XZSHEes11xf210cRhQDobK0tWOh2`

**Fields:**

```json
{
  "globalReviewToken": "wep415okf2einu7xulq4ed",
  "companyInfo": "We are an ai company that provides ai driven marketing automation",
  "updatedAt": {
    "_seconds": 1777638290,
    "_nanoseconds": 709000000
  },
  "icp": "# Ideal Customer Profile (ICP)\n\n## Company Snapshot\n- **Company type:** Refurbished laptop seller in India\n- **Core value proposition:** Provides affordable, quality-checked refurbished laptops for individuals and organizations that want reliable performance at a lower cost than new devices\n- **Primary outcome:** Helps customers access dependable computing while significantly reducing upfront hardware spending\n\n## Ideal Customer\n\n### Market Profile\n- **Geography:** India, especially metro cities, tier 2 cities, education hubs, startup ecosystems, and SME-heavy regions\n- **Customer mix:** B2C and B2B\n- **Buying behavior:** Value-conscious buyers who compare price, specifications, warranty, and seller trust before purchasing\n- **Demand drivers:** Online education, remote work, SME digitization, startup hiring, and budget-sensitive device replacement\n\n## Best-Fit Segments\n\n### 1. Students and Parents\n- Need affordable laptops for classes, assignments, coding, online exams, and general study\n- Compare refurbished business-grade laptops against entry-level new laptops\n- Care about price, battery life, reliability, and warranty\n\n### 2. Young Professionals and Freelancers\n- Need dependable laptops for remote work, coding, design, sales, operations, and meetings\n- Want stronger specifications within a fixed budget\n- Value trusted brands, good condition, and fast delivery\n\n### 3. Small and Medium Businesses (SMEs)\n- Need multiple laptops for employees without high capital expenditure\n- Want standardized devices, GST invoices, and after-sales support\n- Care about reliability, price efficiency, and repeat procurement\n\n### 4. Startups and Bootstrapped Companies\n- Need to equip teams quickly on a limited budget\n- Prefer refurbished premium or business laptops over low-end new consumer models\n- Value bulk pricing, support, and supply consistency\n\n### 5. Schools, Training Centers, and NGOs\n- Need affordable devices in batches for students, trainers, staff, or community programs\n- Want dependable machines with acceptable productivity performance\n- Often buy in bulk and need predictable pricing and service assurance\n\n## Buyer Personas\n\n### Primary B2C Buyer\n**Student / Early-Career Professional**\n- Age 18 to 30\n- Budget-conscious but quality-aware\n- Researches online, checks reviews, and wants the best value for money\n\n### Primary B2B Buyer\n**Founder / Procurement Manager / Admin Head / IT Manager**\n- Responsible for device purchases within budget constraints\n- Wants dependable laptops, fair pricing, and post-purchase support\n- Values seller credibility, billing, and repeatability\n\n### Influencers\n- Parents\n- IT consultants\n- Office administrators\n- Institute heads and trainers\n- Friends, peers, and online reviewers\n\n## Core Pain Points\n- New laptops with decent specifications are too expensive\n- Fear that refurbished laptops may be unreliable or short-lived\n- Uncertainty around battery health, cosmetic condition, and true performance\n- Lack of trust in unorganized sellers or marketplace listings\n- Need for urgent purchase within a fixed budget\n- Concern about warranty, returns, and repair support\n- Difficulty finding branded business-grade laptops at affordable prices\n\n## Jobs to Be Done\n- Buy a dependable laptop within a limited budget\n- Get better specifications than a similarly priced new laptop\n- Equip a team or institution affordably\n- Buy from a trustworthy seller with testing and warranty\n- Reduce hardware cost without sacrificing core productivity needs\n\n## Buying Triggers\n- New academic term or college admission\n- Starting a new job, internship, or freelance role\n- Team expansion or employee onboarding\n- Replacing a broken or outdated laptop quickly\n- Budget cuts or limited capital expenditure\n- Bulk procurement needs for institutions or training programs\n\n## Buying Criteria\n- Competitive pricing and visible savings versus new laptops\n- Clear specifications and honest condition grading\n- Warranty and return policy\n- Battery health and performance assurance\n- Trusted brands like Dell, HP, Lenovo, and Apple\n- Seller credibility, reviews, and responsive customer support\n- PAN-India shipping or local availability\n- GST billing and bulk-order capability for business buyers\n\n## Common Objections\n- Is this laptop genuinely refurbished and fully tested?\n- How long will it last?\n- What is the battery condition?\n- What happens if there is a problem after purchase?\n- Why buy refurbished instead of a cheaper new laptop?\n- Can I trust the seller and the actual device condition?\n\n## Messaging That Resonates\n- **Best value for money:** Better specifications at a much lower price than new laptops\n- **Tested and reliable:** Professionally checked devices backed by support\n- **Warranty-backed peace of mind:** Confidence after purchase\n- **Smart for work and study:** Reliable performance without overspending\n- **Great for teams too:** Affordable bulk sourcing for startups, SMEs, and institutions\n\n## High-Intent Use Cases\n- Student laptops for online classes and assignments\n- Work laptops for remote teams and professionals\n- Budget coding and productivity devices\n- Bulk laptop purchases for startups, schools, and NGOs\n- Urgent replacement laptops for business continuity\n\n## Best Channels to Reach This ICP\n- Google Search for terms like refurbished laptops India, second-hand laptops, used business laptops, and budget laptops for students\n- Instagram and Facebook ads targeting students, parents, freelancers, and SME owners\n- YouTube videos comparing refurbished versus new laptops\n- Marketplace listings with strong ratings, reviews, and warranty messaging\n- LinkedIn outreach for SME and startup bulk sales\n- Partnerships with colleges, coaching centers, training institutes, and local businesses\n\n## Poor-Fit Customers\n- Buyers seeking only the latest-generation premium laptops\n- Customers unwilling to consider refurbished products under any condition\n- Buyers expecting brand-new cosmetic perfection at refurbished pricing\n- Customers who do not value warranty, testing, or trust signals\n\n## ICP Summary\nThe strongest-fit customer is a value-conscious buyer in India, either an individual or a small organization, who needs dependable laptops at a significantly lower cost than new devices. They care most about affordability, trust, warranty, and usable performance. They are most likely to buy when the refurbished option clearly delivers better specifications, credible quality assurance, and dependable after-sales support compared with cheap ."
}
```
